description: Mathematical finance theorems: extended Black-Scholes (put, digitals, vega, rho, forward), Bachelier model, implied volatility, Black-76 formula for futures, single-period binomial replication. All derived from standard Gaussian / discrete-time hypotheses; no Itô calculus required.

theorems.name: Black-Scholes European Put Formula
theorems.description: Discounted expected put payoff under the risk-neutral lognormal hypothesis: P = K e^{-rT} Phi(-d_2) - S_0 Phi(-d_1), where d_1, d_2 are the standard BS quantities.

code.lean:
import MathFin.BlackScholes.Put

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal
open MathFin

variable {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω}

/-- Black-Scholes European put pricing formula via direct integration
    parallel to the call formula (left-tail completing-the-square). -/
theorem bs_put_formula
    {Q : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    {S_0 K r σ T : ℝ} {Z : Ω → ℝ}
    (h : BSCallHyp Q S_0 K r σ T Z) :
    ∫ ω, Real.exp (-r * T) * max (K - bsTerminal S_0 r σ T (Z ω)) 0 ∂Q
      = K * Real.exp (-r * T) * Phi (-(bsd2 S_0 K r σ T)) -
        S_0 * Phi (-(bsd1 S_0 K r σ T)) :=
  MathFin.bs_put_formula h

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Lives in MathFin/BlackScholes/Put.lean (~230 lines). Derivation parallel to bs_call_formula: HasLaw transfer to standard normal, max(K - S_T, 0) as indicator on Iic(-d_2), then left-tail completing-the-square integral_exp_mul_gaussianPDFReal_Iic. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Put-Call Parity
theorems.description: C - P = S_0 - K e^{-rT}: the difference between European call and put prices under the BS hypothesis equals the spot minus the discounted strike. Direct algebraic corollary of bs_call_formula + bs_put_formula + Phi(d) + Phi(-d) = 1.

code.lean:
import MathFin.BlackScholes.Put

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal
open MathFin

variable {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω}

/-- Put-call parity: C - P = S_0 - K e^{-rT}. -/
theorem put_call_parity
    {Q : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    {S_0 K r σ T : ℝ} {Z : Ω → ℝ}
    (h : BSCallHyp Q S_0 K r σ T Z) :
    (∫ ω, Real.exp (-r * T) * max (bsTerminal S_0 r σ T (Z ω) - K) 0 ∂Q) -
    (∫ ω, Real.exp (-r * T) * max (K - bsTerminal S_0 r σ T (Z ω)) 0 ∂Q)
      = S_0 - K * Real.exp (-r * T) :=
  MathFin.bs_put_call_parity h

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Direct algebraic corollary of bs_call_formula + bs_put_formula + Phi_add_Phi_neg. Lives in MathFin/BlackScholes/Put.lean. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Cash-or-Nothing Digital Call
theorems.description: Cash-or-nothing digital pays $1 iff S_T > K. Price: V = e^{-rT} Phi(d_2).

code.lean:
import MathFin.BlackScholes.Digital

open scoped NNReal
open MathFin

variable {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω}

/-- Cash-or-nothing digital call pricing formula. -/

    {Q : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    {S_0 K r σ T : ℝ} {Z : Ω → ℝ}

theorems.name: Asset-or-Nothing Digital Call
theorems.description: Asset-or-nothing digital pays S_T iff S_T > K. Price: V = S_0 Phi(d_1).

theorems.name: Forward / Futures Pricing Formula
theorems.description: F = S_0 e^{rT}: the no-arbitrage forward price equals the risk-neutral expectation of the terminal asset price under BSCallHyp.

code.lean:
import MathFin.BlackScholes.Forward

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal
open MathFin

variable {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω}

/-- No-arbitrage forward price under BS lognormal hypothesis: E_Q[S_T] = S_0 e^{rT}. -/
theorem forward_price
    {Q : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    {S_0 K r σ T : ℝ} {Z : Ω → ℝ}
    (h : BSCallHyp Q S_0 K r σ T Z) :
    ∫ ω, bsTerminal S_0 r σ T (Z ω) ∂Q = S_0 * Real.exp (r * T) :=
  MathFin.expected_terminal_eq_forward h

theorems.name: BS Vega (Sensitivity to Volatility)
theorems.description: Vega = dV/dσ = S phi(d_1) sqrt(T). The Black-Scholes call price has derivative with respect to volatility equal to S * pdf(d_1) * sqrt(T), strictly positive for S, T > 0.

code.lean:
import MathFin.BlackScholes.PDE

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal
open MathFin

/-- Black-Scholes vega: dV/dσ = S phi(d_1) sqrt(τ). -/
theorem bs_vega {K r : ℝ} (hK : 0 < K)
    {S σ τ : ℝ} (hS : 0 < S) (hσ : 0 < σ) (hτ : 0 < τ) :
    HasDerivAt (fun s => MathFin.bsV K r s S τ)
      (S * gaussianPDFReal 0 1 (MathFin.bsd1 S K r σ τ) * Real.sqrt τ) σ :=
  MathFin.hasDerivAt_bsV_sigma hK hS hσ hτ

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Chain rule + magic identity bs_identity collapse the d/dσ d_i terms, leaving S pdf(d_1) sqrt(τ). Lives in MathFin/BlackScholes/PDE.lean. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: BS Rho (Sensitivity to Risk-Free Rate)
theorems.description: Rho = dV/dr = K tau e^{-r tau} Phi(d_2). The Black-Scholes call price derivative with respect to the risk-free rate r equals K * tau * exp(-r*tau) * Phi(d_2).

code.lean:
import MathFin.BlackScholes.PDE

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal
open MathFin

/-- Black-Scholes rho: dV/dr = K tau e^{-r tau} Phi(d_2). -/
theorem bs_rho {K σ τ : ℝ} (hK : 0 < K) (hσ : 0 < σ) (hτ : 0 < τ)
    {S : ℝ} (hS : 0 < S) (r : ℝ) :
    HasDerivAt (fun r' => MathFin.bsV K r' σ S τ)
      (K * τ * Real.exp (-(r * τ)) * MathFin.Phi (MathFin.bsd2 S K r σ τ)) r :=
  MathFin.hasDerivAt_bsV_r hK hσ hτ hS r

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Chain rule on d/dr d_i + d/dr exp(-rτ) + magic identity collapses pdf terms, leaving K*τ*exp(-rτ)*Phi(d_2). Lives in MathFin/BlackScholes/PDE.lean. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Bachelier Model Call Pricing
theorems.description: Under arithmetic-BM dynamics S_T = S_0 + sigma*sqrt(T)*Z (no log, no exponential), the European call price is V = (S_0 - K) Phi(d) + sigma sqrt(T) phi(d), where d = (S_0 - K)/(sigma sqrt(T)).

code.lean:
import MathFin.BlackScholes.Bachelier

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal
open MathFin

variable {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω}

/-- Bachelier European call pricing formula. -/
theorem bachelier_call_formula
    {Q : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    {S_0 K σ T : ℝ} {Z : Ω → ℝ}
    (h : BachelierHyp Q S_0 K σ T Z) :
    ∫ ω, max (bachelierTerminal S_0 σ T (Z ω) - K) 0 ∂Q
      = (S_0 - K) * Phi (bachelierD S_0 K σ T) +
        σ * Real.sqrt T * gaussianPDFReal 0 1 (bachelierD S_0 K σ T) :=
  MathFin.bachelier_call_formula h

metadata.reference: Bachelier (1900) option pricing; modern: Brigo & Mercurio (2006), Black (1976)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Parallels bs_call_formula but for arithmetic BM. Key new primitive: truncated mean of N(0,1): integral_id_mul_gaussianPDFReal_Ioi a = pdf(0,1,a), proved via FTC integral_Ioi_of_hasDerivAt_of_tendsto using (-pdf)' = z*pdf. Plus integrable_id_mul_gaussianPDFReal_volume (transfer via withDensity). Lives in MathFin/BlackScholes/Bachelier.lean (~330 lines). Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Implied Volatility Uniqueness
theorems.description: For S, K, T > 0, the BS call price as a function of sigma is strictly monotone on (0, infinity). Hence the implied volatility (when it exists) is unique.

theorems.name: Black-76 Formula for Futures Options
theorems.description: European call on a futures contract: V = e^{-rT} [F Phi(d_1) - K Phi(d_2)] where d_1 = (log(F/K) + sigma^2 T/2) / (sigma sqrt(T)) and d_2 = d_1 - sigma sqrt(T). Specialization of BS to zero-drift futures + independent discount rate.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Futures.Black76

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal
open MathFin

variable {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω}

/-- Black-76 formula for European call on futures. -/
theorem black_futures_call
    {Q : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    {F K σ T : ℝ} {Z : Ω → ℝ}
    (h : BSCallHyp Q F K 0 σ T Z) (r : ℝ) :
    ∫ ω, Real.exp (-r * T) * max (bsTerminal F 0 σ T (Z ω) - K) 0 ∂Q
      = Real.exp (-r * T) *
          (F * Phi (bsd1 F K 0 σ T) - K * Phi (bsd2 F K 0 σ T)) :=
  MathFin.black_futures_formula h r

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Specialization of bs_call_formula with BS-drift r=0 (futures are martingales) plus external discount rate. Lives in MathFin/Futures/Black76.lean. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Single-Period Binomial Replication Theorem
theorems.description: In a single-period binomial model with no-arbitrage (d < e^r < u), every contingent claim with payoffs (V_u, V_d) in the up/down states is replicable, and the replicating portfolio cost equals the risk-neutral expected payoff discounted: V_0 = e^{-r} (q V_u + (1-q) V_d) where q = (e^r - d) / (u - d).

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof of all three statements: portfolio cost, up-state payoff, down-state payoff. Lives in MathFin/Binomial/Model.lean. Axioms-clean. Multi-period backward induction (binomialPrice) also defined and proved consistent; CRR convergence to BS is upstream.

theorems.name: CRR One-Step Risk-Neutral Martingale Identity
theorems.description: Under CRR parameterization (u_n = e^{σ √Δt}, d_n = e^{-σ √Δt}, Δt = T/n), the risk-neutral up-probability p_n = (e^{rΔt} - d_n)/(u_n - d_n) satisfies p_n · u_n + (1 - p_n) · d_n = e^{rΔt}. The discrete-time discounted asset is a Q-martingale at each step. Exact algebraic identity, not asymptotic.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Binomial.CRRConvergence

open MathFin

/-- CRR one-step risk-neutral martingale identity. -/
theorem crr_one_step_martingale_identity {σ T r : ℝ} {n : ℕ}
    (h_du : crrDown σ T n < crrUp σ T n) :
    crrProb r σ T n * crrUp σ T n + (1 - crrProb r σ T n) * crrDown σ T n
      = Real.exp (crrPerStepRate r T n) :=
  MathFin.crr_one_step_martingale h_du

metadata.reference: CRR parameterization, one-step martingale (Hull 21.1, Shreve I 1.2)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Pure algebraic identity following from the definitions of crrUp, crrDown, crrPerStepRate, crrProb. Lives in MathFin/Binomial/CRRConvergence.lean. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: CRR Risk-Neutral Probability Tends to 1/2
theorems.description: Under CRR parameterization, p_n → 1/2 as n → ∞. This is the substantive analytic step in the CRR-to-BS correspondence: the per-step Bernoulli increment becomes asymptotically symmetric. Implies the variance limit n · σ² Δt · 4 p_n (1 - p_n) → σ² T.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Binomial.CRRConvergence

open MathFin Filter
open scoped Topology

/-- CRR risk-neutral probability tends to 1/2 as n → ∞. -/
theorem crr_prob_tendsto_half {σ T r : ℝ} (hσ : 0 < σ) (hT : 0 < T) :
    Filter.Tendsto (fun n : ℕ => crrProb r σ T n) Filter.atTop (𝓝 (1/2)) :=
  MathFin.crrProb_tendsto_half hσ hT

metadata.reference: CRR convergence (analytic step), Hull 21.1, Shreve I 3.2
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Substantial: ~80 lines using difference-quotient limits (e^{cx}-1)/x → c via hasDerivAt_iff_tendsto_slope, plus quotient-of-limits arithmetic to show p(h) → 1/2 as h → 0, plus composition with h_n = √(T/n). Implies the variance limit. Lives in MathFin/Binomial/CRRConvergence.lean. Axioms-clean. The full distributional convergence binomialPrice → bs_call_price is now proved: see mf-crr-gaussian-limit and mf-crr-bs-call-convergence.

theorems.name: CRR Variance Limit
theorems.description: Under CRR parameterization, n · σ² · (T/n) · 4 p_n (1 - p_n) → σ² T as n → ∞. The per-step variance of the log-return matches the BS variance to leading order. Direct corollary of crrProb_tendsto_half.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Binomial.CRRConvergence

open MathFin Filter
open scoped Topology

/-- CRR variance limit: 4 σ² T · p_n (1 - p_n) → σ² T. -/
theorem crr_variance_limit_theorem {σ T r : ℝ} (hσ : 0 < σ) (hT : 0 < T) :
    Filter.Tendsto
      (fun n : ℕ => 4 * σ^2 * T * (crrProb r σ T n) * (1 - crrProb r σ T n))

theorems.name: CRR to Black-Scholes: Log-Return Convergence in Distribution
theorems.description: Under no-arbitrage at every step, the law of the n-step CRR risk-neutral log-return — the n-fold convolution of the two-point per-step law — converges weakly to the Black-Scholes normal N((r − σ²/2)T, σ²T). The genuine distributional CLT for the binomial tree, via characteristic functions and Lévy's continuity theorem.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Binomial.CRRCharFun

open MathFin Filter
open scoped Topology

/-- CRR → Black–Scholes, convergence in distribution: under no-arbitrage at every
    step (`0 ≤ pₙ ≤ 1`), the `n`-step CRR risk-neutral log-return law — the `n`-fold
    convolution `crrRowProbMeasure` of the two-point per-step law — converges weakly
    to the Black–Scholes normal `N((r − σ²/2)T, σ²T)`. Re-export of
    `MathFin.crr_tendsto_gaussian_inDistribution` (real derivation in
    `MathFin/Binomial/CRRCharFun.lean`: the per-step charFun is computed exactly,
    its `n`-th power converges to the Gaussian charFun
    (`crr_charFun_pow_tendsto_gaussian`), and Lévy's continuity theorem
    (`ProbabilityMeasure.tendsto_of_tendsto_charFun`) upgrades charFun convergence
    to weak convergence, via `charFun_convPow` for the `n`-fold row law). -/
theorem crr_log_return_tendsto_gaussian {r σ T : ℝ} (hσ : 0 < σ) (hT : 0 < T)
    (hp : ∀ n, 0 ≤ crrProb r σ T n ∧ crrProb r σ T n ≤ 1) :
    Tendsto (fun n : ℕ => crrRowProbMeasure r σ T n (hp n).1 (hp n).2) atTop
      (𝓝 (bsLimitProbMeasure r σ T)) :=
  MathFin.crr_tendsto_gaussian_inDistribution hσ hT hp

metadata.reference: CRR to BS distributional convergence (Cox-Ross-Rubinstein 1979; Hull 21.1, Shreve I 3.2)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. The per-step CRR charFun is computed exactly from the two-point step law; its n-th power converges to the Gaussian charFun (crr_charFun_pow_tendsto_gaussian, via real cos/sin splitting of the complex exponential and second-order asymptotics of the CRR parameters); charFun_convPow identifies the n-step row law's charFun as that power; and Lévy's continuity theorem (ProbabilityMeasure.tendsto_of_tendsto_charFun) upgrades charFun convergence to weak convergence of the bundled ProbabilityMeasures. Lives in MathFin/Binomial/CRRCharFun.lean (crr_tendsto_gaussian_inDistribution). Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: CRR to Black-Scholes: Binomial Call Price Convergence (Closed Form)
theorems.description: The n-step Cox-Ross-Rubinstein binomial price of a European call converges, as n → ∞, to the Black-Scholes call price S₀Φ(d₁) − Ke^{−rT}Φ(d₂). The classical CRR→BS convergence theorem, completing the discrete-to-continuous pricing correspondence.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Binomial.CRRClosedForm

open MathFin Filter
open scoped Topology

/-- **Cox–Ross–Rubinstein → Black–Scholes.** Under no-arbitrage at every step, the
    `n`-step CRR binomial price of a European call converges to the literal
    Black–Scholes call price `S₀·Φ(d₁) − K·e^{−rT}·Φ(d₂)`. Re-export of
    `MathFin.binomialPrice_call_tendsto_bs_closed`
    (`MathFin/Binomial/CRRClosedForm.lean`). The route needs no uniform-integrability
    machinery: the bounded *put* payoff passes to the limit under the distributional
    CLT (`binomialPrice_call_tendsto_bs`), binomial put-call parity lifts the limit
    to the unbounded call, and instantiating `bs_put_formula` on the limiting
    Gaussian lands the closed Φ-form. -/
theorem binomial_call_price_tendsto_black_scholes {r σ T S₀ K : ℝ}
    (hσ : 0 < σ) (hT : 0 < T) (hS₀ : 0 < S₀) (hK : 0 < K)
    (hna : ∀ n, BinomialNoArb (crrUp σ T n) (crrDown σ T n) (crrPerStepRate r T n)) :
    Tendsto (fun n : ℕ => binomialPrice (crrUp σ T n) (crrDown σ T n)
        (crrPerStepRate r T n) (fun x => max (x - K) 0) n S₀) atTop
      (𝓝 (S₀ * Phi (bsd1 S₀ K r σ T)
          - K * Real.exp (-(r * T)) * Phi (bsd2 S₀ K r σ T))) :=
  MathFin.binomialPrice_call_tendsto_bs_closed hσ hT hS₀ hK hna

metadata.reference: CRR to BS call-price convergence (Cox-Ross-Rubinstein 1979; Hull 21.1, Shreve I 3.2)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof of the headline CRR→BS convergence. Chain: binomialPrice_eq_integral_convPow identifies the binomial price with a discounted expectation against the n-fold convolution row law; the bounded put payoff passes to the limit under weak convergence (the distributional CLT of mf-crr-gaussian-limit); binomial put-call parity (binomialPrice_callPut_parity) lifts the limit to the unbounded call — no uniform-integrability/Vitali machinery needed; exp_neg_mul_integral_put_gaussian_eq instantiates bs_put_formula on the limiting Gaussian N((r−σ²/2)T, σ²T) through the standardisation Z = (· − (r−σ²/2)T)/(σ√T), and Phi_neg lands the literal closed form. Lives in MathFin/Binomial/CRRCharFun.lean + MathFin/Binomial/CRRClosedForm.lean. Axioms-clean.

    (u d r : ℝ) (g : ℝ → ℝ) (S₀ : ℝ) {N k : ℕ} (hk : k < N) (ω : ℕ → Bool) :

        MathFin.americanPrice u d r g (N - (k + 1))
          (MathFin.spotPath S₀ u d (k + 1) (Function.update ω k true)))
      + (1 - MathFin.crrUpProb u d r) * (Real.exp (-r * (k + 1 : ℕ)) *
        MathFin.americanPrice u d r g (N - (k + 1))
          (MathFin.spotPath S₀ u d (k + 1) (Function.update ω k false)))
      ≤ Real.exp (-r * k) * MathFin.americanPrice u d r g (N - k)
          (MathFin.spotPath S₀ u d k ω) :=
  MathFin.discounted_americanPrice_supermartingale u d r g S₀ hk ω

import MathFin.Binomial.DriftLimit

open MathFin Filter
open scoped Topology

theorem crr_drift_quotient_thm {σ : ℝ} (hσ : σ ≠ 0) (r : ℝ) :
    Tendsto (fun h : ℝ =>
        (2 * Real.exp (r * h^2) - Real.exp (σ * h) - Real.exp (-(σ * h)))
          / (h * (Real.exp (σ * h) - Real.exp (-(σ * h)))))

import MathFin.Actuarial.CompoundPoissonMGF

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped NNReal
open MathFin

/-- **Compound-Poisson aggregate-loss MGF.** For a Poisson claim count `N ∼ Poisson(λ)` and
i.i.d. claim sizes `Xᵢ`, integrating the `n`-claim aggregate MGF against the Poisson law gives
`exp(λ·(M_X(t) − 1))` — the Poisson PGF evaluated at the common claim MGF. Re-exports
`MathFin.compoundPoisson_mgf`. -/
theorem compound_poisson_mgf
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω}
    (lam : ℝ≥0) (t : ℝ) (X : ℕ → Ω → ℝ)
    (hindep : iIndepFun X μ) (hmeas : ∀ i, Measurable (X i))
    (hident : ∀ i, IdentDistrib (X i) (X 0) μ μ) :
    ∫ n, mgf (fun ω => ∑ i ∈ Finset.range n, X i ω) μ t ∂(poissonMeasure lam)
      = Real.exp ((lam : ℝ) * (mgf (X 0) μ t - 1)) :=
  MathFin.compoundPoisson_mgf lam t X hindep hmeas hident

theorems.name: Carr-Madan Spanning Formula
theorems.description: Static replication: any C-two payoff f decomposes as cash + forward + a continuum of OTM puts and calls weighted by f'', over a compact strike range [L,U].

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.CarrMadan

open MathFin

theorem carrMadan_spanning_thm {f f' f'' : ℝ → ℝ} {L U κ S : ℝ}
    (hLκ : L ≤ κ) (hκU : κ ≤ U) (hS : S ∈ Set.Icc L U)
    (hf : ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc L U, HasDerivAt f (f' t) t)
    (hf' : ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc L U, HasDerivAt f' (f'' t) t)
    (hf'' : ContinuousOn f'' (Set.Icc L U)) :
    f S = f κ + f' κ * (S - κ)
      + (∫ K in L..κ, f'' K * max (K - S) 0)
      + (∫ K in κ..U, f'' K * max (S - K) 0) :=
  MathFin.carrMadan_spanning hLκ hκU hS hf hf' hf''

theorems.name: Newton-Raphson Iteration: Local Quadratic Convergence
theorems.description: Local quadratic convergence of Newton–Raphson at a simple root: one step contracts quadratically at the sharp Newton–Kantorovich constant, |newtonStep f f' x − r| ≤ (L/(2m))·(x − r)², under HasDerivAt f + L-Lipschitz f' + |f'| ≥ m > 0 near r; in the basin |x₀ − r| ≤ δ with L·δ ≤ m the error halves each step, giving (1/2)ⁿ geometric decay and newtonSeq → r.

code.lean:
import MathFin.BlackScholes.NewtonConvergence

open Filter Topology MathFin

theorem newton_quadratic_error_and_tendsto_thm {f f' : ℝ → ℝ} {r δ m L x₀ : ℝ}
    (hroot : f r = 0) (hm0 : 0 < m) (hL : 0 ≤ L)
    (hd : ∀ y ∈ Set.Icc (r - δ) (r + δ), HasDerivAt f (f' y) y)
    (hlip : ∀ y ∈ Set.Icc (r - δ) (r + δ), ∀ w ∈ Set.Icc (r - δ) (r + δ),
      |f' y - f' w| ≤ L * |y - w|)
    (hm : ∀ y ∈ Set.Icc (r - δ) (r + δ), m ≤ |f' y|)
    (hx₀ : |x₀ - r| ≤ δ) (hbasin : L * δ ≤ m) :
    (∀ x ∈ Set.Icc (r - δ) (r + δ),
        |MathFin.newtonStep f f' x - r| ≤ L / (2 * m) * (x - r) ^ 2) ∧
      Tendsto (MathFin.newtonSeq f f' x₀) atTop (𝓝 r) :=
  ⟨fun _ hx => MathFin.newtonStep_quadratic_error hroot hm0 hL hd hlip hm hx,
    MathFin.newtonSeq_tendsto_root hroot hm0 hL hd hlip hm hx₀ hbasin⟩

metadata.formalization_scope: Full derivation (upgraded-properly 2026-06-04, sharpened same day): genuine Newton-Raphson convergence theory replacing the definitional fixed-point unfold. (i) One-step quadratic error |newtonStep x - r| <= (L/(2m))(x-r)^2 — the sharp Newton-Kantorovich constant — under HasDerivAt + L-Lipschitz f' + |f'| >= m > 0 on [r-d, r+d], proved via the integral form of the Taylor remainder: the FTC for the linearization-subtracted auxiliary w ↦ f w - f'(x)w turns the remainder into ∫(f'(t) - f'(x))dt, and the Lipschitz bound, linear in |t - x|, integrates to (L/2)(x-r)^2 (no second derivative anywhere); (ii) basin convergence: |x0 - r| <= d with L*d <= m gives the halving invariant and (1/2)^n geometric decay, hence newtonSeq → r (BlackScholes/NewtonConvergence.lean). In the implied-vol application f' = vega > 0 (bsV_vega_pos) discharges the simple-root hypothesis. The old fixed-point identity remains as the trivial lemma newtonStep_fixed_at_root in ImpliedVolatility.lean. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Put-Call Parity from No-Arbitrage
theorems.description: Under a positive linear pricing functional (state prices q summing to the discount factor and pricing the spot), the call minus put portfolio is worth S0 - K*DF. First-principles parity, no distributional hypothesis.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.NoArbitrageDerivations

open MathFin

theorem putCall_parity_from_no_arbitrage_thm
    {ι : Type*} (s : Finset ι) (q : ι → ℝ) (S : ι → ℝ)
    (DF S₀ K : ℝ)
    (h_bond : ∑ i ∈ s, q i = DF)
    (h_stock : ∑ i ∈ s, q i * S i = S₀) :
    (∑ i ∈ s, q i * max (S i - K) 0) - (∑ i ∈ s, q i * max (K - S i) 0)
      = S₀ - K * DF :=
  MathFin.putCall_parity_from_no_arbitrage s q S DF S₀ K h_bond h_stock

theorems.name: Pricing Kernel: Butterfly No-Arbitrage from FTAP
theorems.description: In a two-state market, the FTAP-derived discounted state prices make call-price butterfly spreads cost ≥ 0 — the canonical static no-arbitrage constraint, derived not assumed.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.PricingKernel

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- **No-arbitrage ⟹ butterfly spreads cost ≥ 0** (Phase 53). For sign data
    `z_up > 0 > z_down`, the discounted FTAP state prices are non-negative, so the
    finite-state call-price functional `K ↦ Σᵢ qᵢ · max(Sᵢ − K, 0)` is convex in
    the strike and the butterfly combination `C(K₁) − 2·C((K₁+K₃)/2) + C(K₃)` has
    non-negative cost. The state prices come from the Phase 37 backward-FTAP EMM
    construction, so this static no-arbitrage constraint is derived, not assumed.
    Re-exports `MathFin.stateprice_call_butterfly_nonneg`. -/
theorem pricing_kernel_butterfly_nonneg
    (z_up z_down rT : ℝ) (h_up : 0 < z_up) (h_down : z_down < 0)
    (S : Fin 2 → ℝ) (K₁ K₃ : ℝ) :
    0 ≤ (∑ i, MathFin.statePrices_two_state z_up z_down rT i * max (S i - K₁) 0)
        - 2 * (∑ i, MathFin.statePrices_two_state z_up z_down rT i *
              max (S i - (K₁ + K₃) / 2) 0)
        + (∑ i, MathFin.statePrices_two_state z_up z_down rT i * max (S i - K₃) 0) :=
  MathFin.stateprice_call_butterfly_nonneg z_up z_down rT h_up h_down S K₁ K₃

theorems.name: Cone-Separation Root (Convex-Duality Unification)
theorems.description: A closed convex cone in (ι → ℝ) disjoint from the standard simplex admits a strictly-positive functional that is ≤ 0 on the cone. This single geometric Hahn–Banach fact is the shared root of three apparently separate pillars: the FTAP (attainable-gains cone ⟹ the EMM), the coherent-risk representation (acceptance cone ⟹ representing density), and superhedging (super-replication cone ⟹ the price bound).

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.ConvexDuality

/-- **Cone-separation root — the shared kernel of the convex-duality unification.**
    A closed convex cone `C ⊆ (ι → ℝ)` disjoint from the standard simplex admits a
    strictly-positive functional `q` that is `≤ 0` on `C`. One Hahn–Banach fact, three
    instances: the FTAP (apply to the attainable-gains cone — the separating `q` is the
    equivalent martingale measure), the coherent-risk representation (apply to the
    acceptance cone — `q` is a representing density), and superhedging (apply to the
    super-replication cone — `q` prices the bound). Re-exports
    `MathFin.exists_pos_separating_of_cone_disjoint_simplex`. -/
theorem convex_duality_root {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [Nonempty ι]
    (C : Set (ι → ℝ)) (hCconv : Convex ℝ C) (hCclosed : IsClosed C)
    (hCcone : ∀ ⦃x⦄, x ∈ C → ∀ ⦃c : ℝ⦄, 0 ≤ c → c • x ∈ C)
    (hdisj : Disjoint (stdSimplex ℝ ι) C) :
    ∃ q : ι → ℝ, (∀ i, 0 < q i) ∧ ∀ v ∈ C, ∑ i, q i * v i ≤ 0 :=
  MathFin.exists_pos_separating_of_cone_disjoint_simplex C hCconv hCclosed hCcone hdisj

Generalizes the subspace separating-dual kernel exists_pos_dual_of_disjoint_stdSimplex (now re-derived from it) to a closed convex cone with a one-sided ≤ 0 bound — the form the acceptance cone (coherent risk) and the super-replication cone (superhedging) need, not just the FTAP gains cone. Proof: geometric_hahn_banach_compact_closed; the cone's nonnegative-scaling homogeneity upgrades the subspace's two-sided vanishing to a one-sided sign bound. No axioms beyond Mathlib defaults.

theorems.name: Coherent Risk Representation (ADEH, finite state)
theorems.description: A coherent risk measure on a finite state space is the supremum of expected loss over a set of representing probability measures. The acceptance set is a closed convex cone, and a rejected position is separated from it by a representing density — the same Hahn–Banach separation the FTAP uses for the equivalent martingale measure. The risk-side face of the convex-duality unification.

code.lean:
import MathFin.RiskMeasures.AcceptanceSet

open scoped BigOperators

/-- **Coherent-risk representation (Artzner–Delbaen–Eber–Heath, finite state).** A coherent risk
    measure `ρ` on `ι → ℝ` is the least upper bound of expected loss `∑ qᵢ (-Xᵢ)` over its representing
    probability measures `q ∈ representingSet ρ`. The separating density realising the bound is the
    point-from-cone Hahn–Banach functional — the risk-side instance of the FTAP's separating root.
    Re-exports `MathFin.coherentRisk_isLUB`. -/
theorem coherent_risk_representation {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [Nonempty ι] {ρ : (ι → ℝ) → ℝ}
    (hρ : MathFin.IsCoherentRisk ρ) (X : ι → ℝ) :
    IsLUB ((fun q => ∑ i, q i * (- X i)) '' MathFin.representingSet ρ) (ρ X) :=
  MathFin.coherentRisk_isLUB hρ X

metadata.reference: Artzner–Delbaen–Eber–Heath 1999 (coherent measures of risk); finite-state representation via convex duality
metadata.formalization_scope: Full proof of the finite-state ADEH representation re-exported from MathFin/RiskMeasures/AcceptanceSet.lean: a coherent risk measure (the four ADEH axioms, bundled as IsCoherentRisk) equals the supremum (stated as IsLUB) of expected loss over its representing probability measures. Closedness of the acceptance set is derived from the axioms (ρ convex hence continuous on ℝ^ι), not assumed. The least-upper-bound direction separates the rejected position X + (ρX-ε)·1 from the acceptance cone via the point-from-cone primitive MathFin.exists_separating_of_not_mem_cone and normalises the separating functional to a representing density — the same Hahn–Banach root as the FTAP. No axioms beyond Mathlib defaults.

import MathFin.RiskMeasures.WorstCaseRisk

/-- **Worst-case loss = sup of expected loss over the probability simplex.** The most conservative

theorems.name: Fundamental Superhedging Bound
theorems.description: In a finite-state market, every equivalent martingale measure prices a claim at most its super-replication cost. The EMM prices of a claim lie below its superhedging price — the dual side of the no-arbitrage pricing interval and the pricing-side companion of the coherent-risk representation.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.SuperhedgingDuality

open scoped BigOperators

/-- **Fundamental superhedging bound.** For an excess-return matrix `z`, any equivalent martingale
    measure `q` and any cost-`x` portfolio `θ` super-replicating the claim `Y`, the EMM price of `Y` is
    at most `x`. Re-exports `MathFin.emm_le_superReplication`. -/
theorem superhedging_emm_bound {M N : ℕ} (z : Fin M → Fin N → ℝ) (Y : Fin N → ℝ)
    {q : Fin N → ℝ} (hq : q ∈ MathFin.EMMSet z) {x : ℝ} {θ : Fin M → ℝ}
    (hsr : MathFin.SuperReplicates z Y x θ) :
    ∑ n, q n * Y n ≤ x :=
  MathFin.emm_le_superReplication z Y hq hsr

metadata.reference: Fundamental theorem of superhedging (EMM bound); the dual side of the no-arbitrage price interval
metadata.formalization_scope: Full proof re-exported from MathFin/Foundations/SuperhedgingDuality.lean: in a finite-state market with excess-return matrix z, every equivalent martingale measure (EMMSet) prices a claim Y at most any super-replication cost x (SuperReplicates). Proof: q-weight the super-replication inequality, then the martingale constraint kills the portfolio term (∑ₙ qₙ zₘₙ = 0) and ∑q = 1 leaves x. The reverse strong-duality equality (superhedging price = sup of EMM prices) needs closedness of the super-replication cone — a polyhedral/Farkas fact absent from Mathlib at this pin — and is a documented follow-up. No axioms beyond Mathlib defaults.

theorems.description: On a finite full-support probability space with a filtration and an adapted scalar discounted price over a finite horizon, the market has no arbitrage ⟺ it admits an equivalent martingale measure. import MathFin.Foundations.FTAPDiscrete

open MeasureTheory

/-- **Finite-Ω multi-period FTAP** (Harrison–Pliska; the finite case of
    Dalang–Morton–Willinger). On a finite full-support probability space with a
    filtration `𝓕` and an adapted scalar discounted price `S` over a finite
    horizon `T`, the market has no arbitrage iff it admits an equivalent
    martingale measure. Backward: the attainable-gains subspace misses the
    standard simplex under no arbitrage, so the separating hyperplane is the EMM.
    Forward: martingale-transform telescoping. Re-exports
    `MathFin.ftap_discrete`. -/

    {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} [MeasurableSingletonClass Ω] (𝓕 : Filtration ℕ mΩ)

    (hS : StronglyAdapted 𝓕 S) (hP : ∀ ω, 0 < P {ω}) :
    MathFin.NoArbitrage 𝓕 P S T ↔ ∃ Q, MathFin.IsEMM 𝓕 P S T Q :=
  MathFin.ftap_discrete 𝓕 P S T hS hP

theorems.name: General-Ω One-Period FTAP (Föllmer–Schied)
theorems.description: On an arbitrary probability space, a single scalar discounted excess return Y ∈ L⁰ admits no arbitrage ⟺ there is an equivalent martingale measure Q ~ P under which Y is integrable with E_Q[Y] = 0. theorems.name: d-Asset One-Period FTAP (Esscher EMM, finite-dimensional market)
theorems.description: On an arbitrary probability space, for a ℝᵈ-valued discounted excess return Y, no arbitrage ⟺ there is an equivalent martingale measure Q ~ P with Y integrable and E_Q[Y] = 0 ∈ ℝᵈ — with no non-redundancy hypothesis. The backward direction is the explicit Esscher / minimal-divergence construction: minimise the smooth convex potential θ ↦ E[log(1 + exp⟪θ,Y⟫)]. The potential is constant along the gains kernel N = {θ : ⟪θ,Y⟫ = 0 a.e.} and coercive on its orthogonal complement Nᗮ, so a minimiser on Nᗮ is automatically global (redundant directions absorbed); its first-order condition E[Y·σ⟪θ₀,Y⟫] = 0 (σ logistic) yields a strictly-positive bounded density.

open MeasureTheory

    measure `Q ~ P` with `Y` integrable and `E_Q[Y] = 0` — with **no non-redundancy
    hypothesis** (redundant assets are absorbed by minimising the potential over the gains
    kernel's orthogonal complement). Backward: the explicit Esscher / minimal-divergence
    EMM from minimising `θ ↦ E[log(1+exp⟪θ,Y⟫)]`. Re-exports
    `MathFin.OnePeriodVector.ftap_one_period_vector` at `F = EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin d)`. -/

    (hY : Measurable Y) :

metadata.reference: d-asset one-period Fundamental Theorem of Asset Pricing (Föllmer–Schied Thm 1.6; Esscher / minimal-entropy martingale measure)
metadata.formalization_scope: Honest derivation of the biconditional NoArbitrage ⟺ ∃ EMM re-exported from MathFin/Foundations/FTAPOnePeriodVector.lean, the FULL unrestricted one-period d-asset FTAP — no non-redundancy hypothesis. The MathFin theorem is stated for a discounted excess return valued in any finite-dimensional real inner-product space F (the ℝᵈ market is F = EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin d)). Scope: one trading period, finite-dimensional market, trivial ℱ₀ (constant θ), arbitrary probability space. Backward direction (NA ⟹ EMM): minimise the smooth convex softplus potential θ ↦ ∫ log(1+exp⟪θ,Y⟫); it is constant along the gains kernel N = {θ : ⟪θ,Y⟫ = 0 a.e.} and, under no arbitrage, coercive on Nᗮ (via the unit-sphere minimum of g(θ)=∫⟪θ,Y⟫⁺ together with N ⊓ Nᗮ = ⊥), so a minimiser on Nᗮ exists and — because the potential is N-translation-invariant — is global over all of F; its first-order condition (differentiation under the integral) is ∫ Y·σ⟪θ₀,Y⟫ = 0, so z = σ⟪θ₀,Y⟫ ∈ (0,1) is a strictly-positive bounded fair density and Q = P.withDensity(z/∫z) is the EMM; integrability is dropped by a bounded-density reduction P̃ = P.withDensity((1+‖Y‖)⁻¹/κ). Redundant assets are thereby absorbed (no quotient by the gains kernel or measurable selection needed). No Hahn–Banach, no L⁰-cone closedness, no measurable selection. Only the general-Ω multi-period DMW remains out of scope. No axioms beyond Mathlib defaults.

theorems.name: Variance Swap: Log-Payoff = Realised-Variance Limit
theorems.description: The log-payoff static-replication strike (2/T)·E[log(F/S_T)] = σ² and the realised-variance equipartition functional E[Σ(Δlog S)²] → σ²·T — two distinct functionals of the BS log-price both recover the model variance.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.VarianceSwapEquivalence

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real

/-- **The two variance-swap characterisations agree** (Phase 45). For Brownian
    dynamics `B` (a `BrownianQuadraticVariation`), the two distinct functionals of
    the BS log-price both recover the model variance: the log-payoff
    static-replication integral `(2/T)·E[log(F/S_T)]` equals `σ²`, AND the
    realised-variance equipartition functional `E[Σₖ (Δlog S)²]` converges to
    `σ²·T`. Both conjuncts are genuine functionals (the second is the honest
    `Tendsto` of the squared-increment sum). Re-exports
    `MathFin.varianceSwap_log_eq_QV_limit_value`. -/
theorem variance_swap_log_eq_qv_limit
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    {B : ℝ → Ω → ℝ} (hB : MathFin.BrownianQuadraticVariation μ B)
    {S_0 : ℝ} (hS : 0 < S_0) (r σ : ℝ) {T : ℝ} (hT : 0 < T) :
    (2 / T) * (∫ z, Real.log ((S_0 * Real.exp (r * T)) /
        (S_0 * Real.exp ((r - σ^2/2) * T + σ * Real.sqrt T * z))) ∂(gaussianReal 0 1))
      = σ ^ 2 ∧
    Filter.Tendsto
      (fun n : ℕ => ∫ ω, ∑ k ∈ Finset.range (n + 1),
        (MathFin.bsLogPrice S_0 r σ B (((k : ℝ) + 1) * T / ((n : ℝ) + 1)) ω -
         MathFin.bsLogPrice S_0 r σ B ((k : ℝ) * T / ((n : ℝ) + 1)) ω) ^ 2 ∂μ)
      Filter.atTop (nhds (σ ^ 2 * T)) :=
  MathFin.varianceSwap_log_eq_QV_limit_value hB hS r σ hT

theorems.name: Reflection Principle (Andre, counting form)
theorems.description: Andre's reflection principle for ±1 random-walk paths: the number of length-n paths that touch a barrier a > 0 and end at b ≤ a equals the number of all paths ending at the reflected endpoint 2a − b. The combinatorial backbone of barrier-option counting in the binomial tree; the hitting condition on the reflected side is discharged by a discrete intermediate-value theorem.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Binomial.PathReflection

/-- **Andre's reflection principle, counting form** (1887). For ±1 walks of
    length `n` (paths `Fin n → Bool`), a barrier `a > 0`, and an endpoint
    `b ≤ a`: the number of paths that touch the barrier and end at `b` equals
    the number of ALL paths ending at the reflected endpoint `2a − b` — the
    hitting condition on the reflected side is automatic by the discrete IVT,
    since `2a − b ≥ a`. This is the counting backbone of barrier-option
    pricing in the binomial tree. Re-exports
    `MathFin.reflectionPrincipleEquiv_below` through `Nat.card_congr`. -/
theorem reflection_principle_count {n : ℕ} (a b : ℤ) (ha : 0 < a) (hb : b ≤ a) :
    Nat.card {ω : Fin n → Bool // MathFin.HitsLevel a ω ∧ MathFin.walkPos ω n = b} =
    Nat.card {ω : Fin n → Bool // MathFin.walkPos ω n = 2 * a - b} :=
  Nat.card_congr (MathFin.reflectionPrincipleEquiv_below a b ha hb)

theorems.name: Vasicek SDE Terminal Distribution is Gaussian (derived)
theorems.description: The Vasicek short-rate SDE dr = κ(θ − r)dt + σ dB has terminal solution r_T = r₀e^{−κT} + θ(1 − e^{−κT}) + σ ∫₀ᵀ e^{−κ(T−s)} dB_s, and this theorem DERIVES (no longer posits) its terminal law r_T ~ N(vasicekSDEMean, vasicekSDEVariance) = N(r₀e^{−κT}+θ(1−e^{−κT}), σ²(1−e^{−2κT})/(2κ)). The stochastic term is the Wiener integral of the DETERMINISTIC integrand e^{−κ(T−s)}, hence Gaussian (sc-wiener-integral-gaussian) with variance its squared L²-norm ∫₀ᵀ e^{−2κ(T−s)} ds = (1−e^{−2κT})/(2κ) (an FTC computation); the affine map x ↦ mean + σx then sends N(0, ∫kernel²) to N(mean, σ²·∫kernel²) = N(vasicekSDEMean, vasicekSDEVariance) via gaussianReal_const_mul / gaussianReal_const_add. This is the FIRST consumer of the deterministic-integrand Itô tower in the FixedIncome layer — the analytic Wiener/Itô machinery made load-bearing in a pricing module, closing the two-tower gap for the Vasicek short rate (VasicekSDE.lean previously stated this law without derivation).

code.lean:
import MathFin.FixedIncome.VasicekSDEGaussian

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped NNReal
open MathFin MathFin.WienerIntegralL2

theorem vasicek_sde_terminal_gaussian
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    {B : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ} (hB : IsPreBrownianReal B μ)
    (r₀ θ σ : ℝ) {κ : ℝ} (hκ : 0 < κ) (T : ℝ≥0) :
    HasLaw (fun ω => vasicekSDEMean r₀ θ κ (T : ℝ)
        + σ * (wienerIntegralLp B hB T (vasicekKernelLp κ hκ.le T) ω))
      (gaussianReal (vasicekSDEMean r₀ θ κ (T : ℝ))
        (vasicekSDEVariance σ κ (T : ℝ)).toNNReal) μ :=
  MathFin.vasicekShortRate_hasLaw_gaussian hB r₀ θ σ hκ T

theorems.name: Change of Numéraire (Price Invariance)
theorems.description: Given a pricing measure Q for the money-market numéraire B and a second strictly-positive numéraire N, the numéraire measure Q^N (Radon–Nikodym density dQ^N/dQ = (N_T·B₀)/(N₀·B_T)) reprices every terminal claim X consistently: N₀·E^{Q^N}[X/N_T] = B₀·E^Q[X/B_T]. This is the abstract change-of-numéraire theorem — the shared backbone of the library's concrete numéraire changes (the Black–Scholes stock numéraire, the exchange-option second-asset numéraire, the Garman forward/annuity normal form).

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.Numeraire

open MeasureTheory

/-- **Change of numéraire — price is numéraire-invariant.**
Given a pricing measure `Q` for the money-market numéraire `B` and a second
strictly-positive numéraire `N`, the numéraire measure
`Q^N = numeraireMeasure Q B_T N_T B₀ N₀` (density `dQ^N/dQ = (N_T·B₀)/(N₀·B_T)`)
reprices every terminal claim `X` consistently:

  `N₀·𝔼^{Q^N}[X/N_T] = B₀·𝔼^Q[X/B_T]`.

The proof is a one-line density cancellation, and needs no integrability
hypothesis. This is the abstract backbone of the library's concrete numéraire
changes (the BS stock numéraire, the exchange-option second-asset numéraire, the
Garman forward/annuity numéraires). Re-exports `MathFin.changeOfNumeraire`. -/
theorem change_of_numeraire {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] (Q : Measure Ω)
    (X BT NT : Ω → ℝ) (B0 N0 : ℝ)
    (hNTmeas : Measurable NT) (hBTmeas : Measurable BT)
    (hNTpos : ∀ ω, 0 < NT ω) (hBTpos : ∀ ω, 0 < BT ω) (hB0 : 0 ≤ B0) (hN0 : 0 < N0) :
    N0 * ∫ ω, X ω / NT ω ∂(MathFin.numeraireMeasure Q BT NT B0 N0)
      = B0 * ∫ ω, X ω / BT ω ∂Q :=
  MathFin.changeOfNumeraire X hNTmeas hBTmeas hNTpos hBTpos hB0 hN0

The identity is a pure measure-transport fact (integral_withDensity_eq_integral_toReal_smul) plus scalar cancellation of N_T — it holds for every claim X with NO integrability or square-integrability hypothesis, needing only strict positivity of the two numéraires. Companion numeraireMeasure_isProbabilityMeasure shows Q^N is a probability measure under the tradeability/normalization condition E^Q[N_T/B_T] = N₀/B₀ (N/B a Q-martingale). The abstract backbone is CONSUMED by the Black–Scholes stock numéraire: stockNumeraireMeasure_eq_numeraireMeasure (MathFin/BlackScholes/StockNumeraire.lean) exhibits dQ^(S)/dQ = e^{−rT}·S_T/S₀ as the instance B_T = e^{rT}, B₀ = 1, N = S. theorems.name: Kelly Numéraire Portfolio ⟹ EMM (Price Invariance, discrete)
theorems.description: The growth-optimal (Kelly) portfolio is the numéraire portfolio: deflating the physical measure by the growth-optimal terminal wealth turns it into the equivalent martingale measure. In the two-outcome bet, the GOP-deflated probabilities q₊ = p/W*₊ = 1/(b+1), q₋ = (1-p)/W*₋ = b/(b+1) (independent of the physical p) sum to 1 and make the bet a martingale (q₊·b + q₋·(-1) = 0). The p-independence is exactly the Kelly first-order condition. The discrete numéraire-portfolio ⟹ EMM identity.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Performance.KellyNumeraire

open MathFin

/-- **The Kelly (growth-optimal) portfolio as numéraire induces the risk-neutral
measure.** In the two-outcome market (bet at fraction `f` pays odds `b` with physical
probability `p`), deflate the physical measure by the growth-optimal terminal wealth
`W*₊ = 1 + f*·b`, `W*₋ = 1 - f*` at the Kelly fraction `f* = kellyFraction p b`. The
resulting probabilities `q₊ = p/W*₊`, `q₋ = (1-p)/W*₋` make the bet a martingale — zero
expected excess return `q₊·b + q₋·(-1) = 0` — i.e. the GOP-deflated measure is the EMM.
The discrete numéraire-portfolio ⟹ EMM identity. Re-exports
`MathFin.kellyNumeraire_isRiskNeutral`. -/
theorem kelly_numeraire_isRiskNeutral {p b : ℝ} (hp : 0 < p) (hp1 : p < 1) (hb : 0 < b) :
    p / (1 + kellyFraction p b * b) * b
      + (1 - p) / (1 - kellyFraction p b) * (-1) = 0 :=
  MathFin.kellyNumeraire_isRiskNeutral hp hp1 hb

metadata.reference: Numéraire portfolio / benchmark approach (Long 1990; Platen); Kelly growth-optimal criterion
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. Re-export of MathFin.kellyNumeraire_isRiskNeutral (MathFin/Performance/KellyNumeraire.lean): the growth-optimal (Kelly) portfolio, used as numéraire, deflates the physical measure into the EMM. Consumes the Kelly fraction kellyFraction and its first-order optimality (kellyGrowth_deriv_at_kelly): 1 + f*·b = p·(b+1), so the GOP-deflated win-probability p/W*₊ = 1/(b+1) is p-independent — the fair-odds risk-neutral probability. HONEST SCOPE: the discrete, two-outcome market — the fully elementary shadow of the continuous Long/Platen benchmark theorem (deflated prices are P-martingales, EMM density ∝ 1/N*), which needs a state-price-density / market model absent from the Itô tower. No axioms beyond Mathlib defaults.

theorems.name: Exchange Option in the S²-Numéraire (change-of-numéraire instance)
theorems.description: Margrabe's exchange option valued in the second-asset (S²) numéraire is a genuine instance of the general change of numéraire: S²₀·E^{Q^(S²)}[max(S¹/S²−1,0)] = E^Q[max(S¹−S²,0)], where Q^(S²) = numeraireMeasure Q 1 S²_T 1 S²₀. Composes changeOfNumeraire (X = the exchange payoff, N = S²) with the payoff reduction max(S¹−S²,0) = S²·max(S¹/S²−1,0).

code.lean:
import MathFin.BlackScholes.ExchangeOption

open MeasureTheory MathFin

/-- **The exchange option in the S²-numéraire is a change-of-numéraire instance.** With
the second asset `S²` as numéraire (`numeraireMeasure Q 1 S²_T 1 S²₀`, density
`dQ^(S²)/dQ = S²_T/S²₀`), the option's `S²`-numéraire value equals its value under the
reference measure `Q`:
`S²₀·𝔼^{Q^(S²)}[max(S¹_T/S²_T − 1, 0)] = 𝔼^Q[max(S¹_T − S²_T, 0)]`. This is the general
`changeOfNumeraire` law at `X =` the exchange payoff, `N = S²`, composed with the payoff
reduction `max(S¹−S²,0) = S²·max(S¹/S²−1,0)`; no integrability hypothesis is needed. Re-
exports `MathFin.exchangeOption_numeraire_price`. -/
theorem exchange_option_numeraire_price {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω}
    (Q : Measure Ω) (S1T S2T : Ω → ℝ) (S2_0 : ℝ)
    (hS2meas : Measurable S2T) (hS2pos : ∀ ω, 0 < S2T ω) (hS20 : 0 < S2_0) :
    S2_0 * ∫ ω, max (S1T ω / S2T ω - 1) 0
        ∂(MathFin.numeraireMeasure Q (fun _ => 1) S2T 1 S2_0)
      = ∫ ω, max (S1T ω - S2T ω) 0 ∂Q :=
  MathFin.exchangeOption_numeraire_price Q S1T S2T S2_0 hS2meas hS2pos hS20

theorems.name: Vasicek Zero-Coupon Bond Price (Affine Term Structure)
theorems.description: The Vasicek short-rate model prices the zero-coupon bond as P(0,T) = E[exp(-∫₀ᵀ r_s ds)], the risk-neutral present value of one unit at T. Under Vasicek the integrated short rate ∫₀ᵀ r_s ds = M(T) + σ∫₀ᵀ g dB is again Gaussian (integrated OU kernel g(u) = (1-e^{-κ(T-u)})/κ), so the bond price is a Gaussian Laplace transform and collapses to the classical AFFINE TERM STRUCTURE P(0,T) = A(T)·exp(-B(T)·r₀) with B(T) = (1-e^{-κT})/κ. The exponential-affine dependence on the initial short rate r₀ is the defining property of an affine term-structure model.

code.lean:
import MathFin.FixedIncome.VasicekBondPrice

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped NNReal
open MathFin MathFin.WienerIntegralL2

/-- **Vasicek zero-coupon bond price — the affine term structure.**
The risk-neutral present value `P(0,T) = 𝔼[exp(−∫₀ᵀ r_s ds)]` of one unit paid
at `T` under the Vasicek short-rate model separates the initial rate into the
exponential-affine form `P(0,T) = A(T)·exp(−B(T)·r₀)`, `B(T) = (1−e^{−κT})/κ`.
Re-exports `MathFin.vasicekBondPrice_affine`. -/
theorem vasicek_bond_price_affine
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    {B : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ} (hB : IsPreBrownianReal B μ)
    (r₀ θ σ : ℝ) {κ : ℝ} (hκ : 0 < κ) (T : ℝ≥0) :
    ∫ ω, Real.exp (-(vasicekIntegratedRate hB r₀ θ σ hκ T ω)) ∂μ
      = vasicekBondA θ σ κ (T : ℝ) * Real.exp (-(vasicekBondB κ (T : ℝ) * r₀)) :=
  MathFin.vasicekBondPrice_affine hB r₀ θ σ hκ T

The Vasicek zero-coupon bond price P(0,T) = E[exp(-int_0^T r_s ds)] as the Gaussian Laplace transform of the integrated short rate, collapsing to the affine term structure P = A(T)*exp(-B(T)*r0), B(T) = (1-e^{-kappa T})/kappa. The integrated rate int_0^T r_s ds = M(T) + sigma*int_0^T g dB is carried in its Wiener representation (integrated OU kernel g(u) = (1-e^{-kappa(T-u)})/kappa; the deterministic time-order swap is the modelling bridge, cited not proven -- parity with the OU-solution model of mf-vasicek-sde-terminal-gaussian). theorems.name: Forward Price under the T-Forward Measure
theorems.description: The T-forward measure Q^T is the pricing measure with the zero-coupon bond P(·,T) as numéraire. Its defining property is that the forward price is the Q^T-expectation of the spot: E^{Q^T}[S_T] = S_0/P(0,T) = S_0·e^{rT} = F(0,T), the no-arbitrage forward price. Built as a change-of-numéraire instance (bond slots N_T = P(T,T) = 1, N_0 = P(0,T) = e^{-rT}, money-market reference B_T = e^{rT}, B_0 = 1), the natural next numéraire instance after the stock and exchange-option S²-numéraires.

its `Q^T`-expectation: `𝔼^{Q^T}[S_T] = S_0·e^{rT} = S_0/P(0,T) = F(0,T)`, the

theorems.name: Two-Date Geometric-Asian Log-Driver is Gaussian
theorems.description: The geometric-average Asian option's log-driver at two sampling dates s <= t is Gaussian: the average of the Brownian values (B_s + B_t)/2 -- the Gaussian part of log sqrt(S_s * S_t) under GBM -- has the centred Gaussian law N(0, (3s+t)/4), the variance being the covariance sum (s + 2*min(s,t) + t)/4. Turns the geometric average into a priceable lognormal, complementing the AM-GM payoff bound (mf-asian-geom-le-arith-two). The derivation reads the sum of Brownian values as a single Wiener integral of the deterministic step kernel 1/2*(1_{(0,s]} + 1_{(0,t]}) via the new identity int 1_{(0,u]} dB = B_u, so wienerIntegralLp_hasLaw_gaussian gives the Gaussian law and the variance is the kernel L2-norm evaluated through the Brownian covariance int B_s * B_t = min(s,t).

import MathFin.BlackScholes.AsianGeometric

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped NNReal
open MathFin MathFin.WienerIntegralL2

/-- **Two-date geometric-Asian log-driver is Gaussian.**
For a Black–Scholes GBM `S_u = S₀·exp((r−σ²/2)u + σ B_u)` sampled at two dates
`s ≤ t`, the geometric average `√(S_s·S_t)` is lognormal because its log-driver —
the average of the Brownian values `(B_s + B_t)/2` — is Gaussian with the
covariance-sum variance `(s + 2·min(s,t) + t)/4 = (3s+t)/4`.
Re-exports `MathFin.asianGeom_driver_hasLaw`. -/
theorem asian_geom_driver_gaussian
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    {B : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ} (hB : IsPreBrownianReal B μ) (T : ℝ≥0)
    {s t : ℝ≥0} (hst : s ≤ t) (hsT : s ≤ T) (htT : t ≤ T) :
    HasLaw (fun ω => (B s ω + B t ω) / 2)
      (gaussianReal 0 ((3 * (s : ℝ) + t) / 4).toNNReal) μ :=
  MathFin.asianGeom_driver_hasLaw hB T hst hsT htT

open MathFin MathFin.WienerIntegralL2

/-- **n-date geometric-Asian log-driver is Gaussian.** The average of the Brownian
values `(1/n) ∑ᵢ B_{τᵢ}` — the Gaussian part of the log geometric average of a
Black–Scholes GBM sampled at `τ₁,…,τₙ` — has the centred Gaussian law
`N(0, (1/n²) ∑ᵢ∑ⱼ min(τᵢ,τⱼ))`. Re-exports `MathFin.geomAsianN_driver_hasLaw`. -/
theorem asian_geom_n_driver
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    {B : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ} (hB : IsPreBrownianReal B μ) (T : ℝ≥0) {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n)
    (τ : Fin n → ℝ≥0) (hτT : ∀ i, τ i ≤ T) :
    HasLaw (fun ω => (∑ i, B (τ i) ω) / n)
      (gaussianReal 0 ((∑ i, ∑ j, min (τ i : ℝ) (τ j)) / (n : ℝ) ^ 2).toNNReal) μ :=
  MathFin.geomAsianN_driver_hasLaw hB T hn τ hτT

code.lean:
import MathFin.BlackScholes.AsianGeometricN

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal
open MathFin MathFin.WienerIntegralL2

/-- **n-date geometric-Asian call price, closed form.** The discounted expected
geometric-Asian call payoff equals the Black–Scholes call price with the effective
spot `Ŝ₀` and volatility `σ_G` — the geometric average reduced to one effective BS
driver. Re-exports `MathFin.geomAsianN_call_price`. -/
theorem asian_geom_n_price
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    {B : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ} (hB : IsPreBrownianReal B μ) (T : ℝ≥0) {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n)
    (τ : Fin n → ℝ≥0) (hτT : ∀ i, τ i ≤ T)
    (S₀ K r σ Tmat : ℝ) (hS₀ : 0 < S₀) (hK : 0 < K) (hσ : 0 < σ) (hTmat : 0 < Tmat)
    (hV : 0 < geomAsianVar τ) :
    ∫ ω, Real.exp (-r * Tmat) *
        max ((∏ i, (S₀ * Real.exp ((r - σ ^ 2 / 2) * (τ i : ℝ) + σ * B (τ i) ω)) ^ ((n : ℝ)⁻¹))
          - K) 0 ∂μ
      = geomAsianEffSpot S₀ r σ Tmat τ *
          Phi (bsd1 (geomAsianEffSpot S₀ r σ Tmat τ) K r (geomAsianEffVol σ Tmat τ) Tmat)
        - K * Real.exp (-r * Tmat) *
          Phi (bsd2 (geomAsianEffSpot S₀ r σ Tmat τ) K r (geomAsianEffVol σ Tmat τ) Tmat) :=
  MathFin.geomAsianN_call_price hB T hn τ hτT S₀ K r σ Tmat hS₀ hK hσ hTmat hV

theorems.name: Reflection-Principle Counting Identity
theorems.description: Number of paths touching a and ending at b equals number ending at 2a-b, for 0<a, b<=a.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Binomial.BarrierReflection

open MathFin

/-- **Reflection-principle counting identity** (barrier-pricing backbone). For a barrier
`0 < a` and terminal `b ≤ a`, the number of `n`-step ±1 paths that touch `a` and finish
at `b` equals the number that finish at `2a − b`. Re-exports `MathFin.reflection_principle_card`. -/
theorem barrier_reflection_count (n : ℕ) (a b : ℤ) (ha : 0 < a) (hb : b ≤ a) :
    Nat.card {ω : Fin n → Bool // HitsLevel a ω ∧ walkPos ω n = b}
      = Nat.card {ω : Fin n → Bool // walkPos ω n = 2 * a - b} :=
  MathFin.reflection_principle_card (n := n) a b ha hb

theorems.name: Binomial Maximal-Distribution (Running-Max Law)
theorems.description: Number of paths with running max >= a equals 2*#{end>a} + #{end=a}.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Binomial.BarrierReflection

open MathFin

/-- **Binomial maximal-distribution identity** (running-maximum law). For a barrier `0 < a`,
the number of `n`-step ±1 paths whose running maximum reaches `a` is
`2·#{end > a} + #{end = a}` — the discrete `P(max ≥ a) = 2·P(Sₙ > a) + P(Sₙ = a)`.
Re-exports `MathFin.binomial_maximal_distribution_card`. -/
theorem barrier_maximal_distribution (n : ℕ) (a : ℤ) (ha : 0 < a) :
    Nat.card {ω : Fin n → Bool // HitsLevel a ω}
      = 2 * Nat.card {ω : Fin n → Bool // a < walkPos ω n}
        + Nat.card {ω : Fin n → Bool // walkPos ω n = a} :=
  MathFin.binomial_maximal_distribution_card (n := n) a ha

theorems.name: Quanto Forward Derived from a Joint-Gaussian FX Model
theorems.description: FX-density-weighted foreign expectation of the terminal equals quantoForward; the -rho sigma_S sigma_FX drift is derived, not posited.

code.lean:
import MathFin.BlackScholes.QuantoGrounding

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal
open MathFin

/-- **Girsanov-grounded quanto forward.** The FX-density-weighted foreign expectation of the
foreign terminal equals the quanto-adjusted forward `S₀·exp((r_dom − ρ σ_S σ_FX)T)` — the
`−ρ σ_S σ_FX` drift adjustment is the log-covariance produced by the change of measure, not an
assumption. Re-exports `MathFin.quantoForward_of_gaussian`. -/
theorem quanto_forward_grounded
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    (S₀ r_dom σ_S σ_FX ρ T : ℝ) (hT : 0 ≤ T) (hρ : ρ ^ 2 ≤ 1)
    {Z_S Z_perp : Ω → ℝ}
    (hZS : HasLaw Z_S (gaussianReal 0 1) μ) (hZperp : HasLaw Z_perp (gaussianReal 0 1) μ)
    (hZSm : Measurable Z_S) (hZperpm : Measurable Z_perp)
    (hindep : IndepFun Z_S Z_perp μ) :
    ∫ ω, (S₀ * Real.exp ((r_dom - σ_S ^ 2 / 2) * T + σ_S * Real.sqrt T * Z_S ω))
        * Real.exp (-(σ_FX * Real.sqrt T)
            * (ρ * Z_S ω + Real.sqrt (1 - ρ ^ 2) * Z_perp ω) - σ_FX ^ 2 * T / 2) ∂μ
      = quantoForward S₀ r_dom ρ σ_S σ_FX T :=
  MathFin.quantoForward_of_gaussian S₀ r_dom σ_S σ_FX ρ T hT hρ hZS hZperp hZSm hZperpm hindep

theorems.description: The quadratic ansatz θ̌(t,q)=−A(t)q²−B(t)q−C(t) solves the approximate (quadratic-Hamiltonian) Hamilton–Jacobi equation when (A,B,C) solve the Riccati/linear ODE system.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.MarketMakingRiccati

/-- **Market-making value function — approximate-HJ verification (d = 1).** The quadratic ansatz
`θ̌(t,q) = −A(t) q² − B(t) q − C(t)` has time-derivative equal to the approximate
(quadratic-Hamiltonian) Hamilton–Jacobi right-hand side when `(A, B, C)` solve the scalar
Riccati/linear ODE system. Re-exports `MathFin.valueFunction_satisfies_approxHJ`. -/
theorem mm_value_function_hj
    (γ σ z α₀b α₁b α₂b α₀a α₁a α₂a : ℝ) (hz : 0 < z) (A B C : ℝ → ℝ) (t q : ℝ)
    (hA : HasDerivAt A (2 * z * (α₂b + α₂a) * A t ^ 2 - γ * σ ^ 2 / 2) t)
    (hB : HasDerivAt B (2 * z * (α₁b - α₁a) * A t + 2 * z ^ 2 * (α₂b - α₂a) * A t ^ 2
                        + 2 * z * (α₂b + α₂a) * A t * B t) t)
    (hC : HasDerivAt C (z * (α₀b + α₀a) + z ^ 2 * (α₁b + α₁a) * A t + z * (α₁b - α₁a) * B t
                        + z ^ 3 * (α₂b + α₂a) * A t ^ 2 / 2 + z * (α₂b + α₂a) * B t ^ 2 / 2
                        + z ^ 2 * (α₂b - α₂a) * A t * B t) t) :
    HasDerivAt (fun s => -(A s) * q ^ 2 - B s * q - C s)
      (γ * σ ^ 2 / 2 * q ^ 2 - z * (α₀b + α₀a)
        - α₁b * (2 * A t * q * z + A t * z ^ 2 + B t * z)
        - α₁a * (-2 * A t * q * z + A t * z ^ 2 - B t * z)
        - (1 / (2 * z)) * (α₂b * (2 * A t * q * z + A t * z ^ 2 + B t * z) ^ 2
                    + α₂a * (-2 * A t * q * z + A t * z ^ 2 - B t * z) ^ 2)) t :=
  MathFin.valueFunction_satisfies_approxHJ γ σ z α₀b α₁b α₂b α₀a α₁a α₂a hz A B C t q hA hB hC

theorems.name: Market-Making Closed-Form Quotes — Constant Spread + Linear Skew
theorems.description: The greedy quotes give a constant half-spread A(t)z+c (c=quoteConstA for Model A, quoteConstB for Model B) and an inventory-linear model-independent skew −2A(t)q−B(t).

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.MarketMakingRiccati

/-- **Market-making closed-form quotes — constant spread + linear skew (Models A & B).** The greedy
half-spread is constant in inventory (with the model constant `c`: `quoteConstA` for the CARA
Model A, `quoteConstB` for the risk-adjusted Model B), and the skew is inventory-linear and
model-independent (`c` cancels). Re-exports `MathFin.mmHalfSpread_const` / `MathFin.mmSkew_linear`. -/
theorem mm_quotes_spread_skew (A B : ℝ → ℝ) (γ z k t q : ℝ) :
    (MathFin.mmQuoteAsk A B z (MathFin.quoteConstA γ z k) t q
        + MathFin.mmQuoteBid A B z (MathFin.quoteConstA γ z k) t q) / 2
      = A t * z + MathFin.quoteConstA γ z k
    ∧ (MathFin.mmQuoteAsk A B z (MathFin.quoteConstB k) t q
        + MathFin.mmQuoteBid A B z (MathFin.quoteConstB k) t q) / 2
      = A t * z + MathFin.quoteConstB k
    ∧ ∀ c : ℝ, (MathFin.mmQuoteAsk A B z c t q - MathFin.mmQuoteBid A B z c t q) / 2
      = -2 * A t * q - B t :=
  ⟨MathFin.mmHalfSpread_const A B z (MathFin.quoteConstA γ z k) t q,
   MathFin.mmHalfSpread_const A B z (MathFin.quoteConstB k) t q,
   fun c => MathFin.mmSkew_linear A B z c t q⟩

theorem mf_performance_upside_capture {S : Type*} (up : Finset S) (p b : S → ℝ) (c : ℝ)
    (h : (∑ s ∈ up, b s) ≠ 0) : upCapture up (fun s => c * p s) b = c * upCapture up p b :=
  MathFin.upCapture_scale_invariant up p b c h
